authored_by: Watson (APM) — V20 doctrine v3.8 / SKILL v2.8 / AJ SOP v2.1 / STAGE PROGRESSION SOP v1.0

header.primary_setup: Demand-Driven EPSU — WFE cyclical recovery confirmed by Q1-26 orders +22.3% YoY at book-to-bill 1.4; AI-driven logic/HBM capex pulling TSMC ($52-56bn FY26 capex) + Samsung + Intel WFE spend forward. PCT segment recovering from CHF 193m FY23 trough → CHF 247m FY25 → CHF 280-310m feasible FY26.
header.primary_setup_verdict: C — present and verifiable but contested. Recovery confirmed in orders, not yet in revenue. AEIS divergence (+21% vs COMET +7.3% cc FY25) raises structural-share-loss question.
header.secondary_setup: Corporate Change EPSU/EPT — CEO Haferl (Sept-22, 3.5y in role, internal X-ray business head) + CFO Witt (Jan-25, ex-LPKF Laser, cost-discipline reputation) + Penang Malaysia capacity (CHF 42.5m capex FY25, FY27+ benefit) + IXS restructuring (CHF 15-20m charges, breakeven FY26 if European industrial stabilises).
header.conviction: C — forming. WFE recovery in orders is real; margin expansion path arithmetically credible (30-35% incremental margins); but AEIS competitive dynamics unresolved + CHF structural headwind permanent + management credibility dented by FY25 guidance miss + CMD downgrade.
header.time_horizon: N1-3Y; fulcrum events inside 12M: H1-26 revenue tracking (CHF 240m+ confirms recovery momentum) + AEIS competitive intelligence resolution + IXS breakeven trajectory.

A.title: FINANCIALS
A.inline_verdict: FY25 trough confirmed: revenue CHF 457m, EBITDA margin 10.1%, EPS -3.71 adj. Q4-25 H2 inflection: revenue +11% HoH, EBITDA margin +300bp HoH to ~11.5%. Operating leverage confirmed when volumes accelerate. CHF appreciation (-11-12% FY25 revenue headwind) is a permanent drag, not temporary.

rows.label: Revenue
rows.delta: +7.3% cc; FY25 missed mid-cycle target by -16% (CMD cut 800-900m → 670-770m)

items.text: FY25 revenue split: PCT (RF plasma control) 61% = CHF 279m, IXS (industrial X-ray) 39% = CHF 178m. PCT down from CHF 247m mid-cycle expected; IXS in restructuring.
**FY25 miss drivers:** (i) CHF appreciation -11-12% revenue headwind (unhedgeable, permanent), (ii) IXS underperformance (-6.8% segment EBITDA margin), (iii) Penang capex peak.

sub.text: Not earnings deterioration per se — cycle trough + one-off investments. H2-25 inflection +300bp confirms operating leverage when volumes accelerate.

items.text: **The financial picture is __cyclical-trough-with-confirmed-recovery-in-orders__** — but not yet in revenue. Q4-25 H2 print is the structural positive; FY26 guidance is the credibility test.

blocks.text: _Mgmt CMD Nov-25 cut peak revenue target from CHF 800-900m to CHF 670-770m (-16%). Credibility-event guidance reset; FY26 guidance qualitative ('positive cc growth')._

items.text: FY26 guidance: net sales 'positive cc growth' (qualitative); EBITDA margin 11-13% range; not quantitative on EPS or EBIT.
CMD Nov-25 medium-term: peak revenue CHF 670-770m by 2027-28 cycle peak (cut from 800-900m). EBITDA margin 14-16% target.

sub.text: Implies 2x revenue growth from FY25 trough + 4-6pp margin expansion. Aggressive but not implausible if WFE cycle sustains.

items.text: **Guidance credibility = C.** FY24 delivered at midpoint ✓; FY25 missed by ~6% ✗; CMD cut peak target -16%. Two strikes; vague FY26 guidance is defensive, not confident.

blocks.text: _SS coverage GAP — AS source returned no broker data. Implied consensus FY26 EPS CHF 11; FY27 EPS CHF 10-12. Limited coverage = potential inefficiency if thesis works._

items.text: Sell-side coverage notably thin for a CHF 1.2bn Swiss mid-cap — 4-6 active analysts (Vontobel, ZKB, BoA, Kepler — NOT confirmed in AS query).
Implicit consensus FY26 EPS CHF 11 (post-CMD reset); FY27 EPS CHF 10-12. Wide dispersion not visible due to coverage gap.
**Coverage gap = double-edged.** Could signal inefficiency (limited research → mispriced); could signal disinterest (mid-cap, illiquid, niche). Triaging cannot resolve; ESA via 06-SS deep query.

blocks.text: _Modal (Watson APM): FY26 revenue CHF 480m (+5% cc), EBITDA margin 12.5%, EPS CHF 5. Conservative vs CMD trajectory; aligned with mid of consensus range._

rows.label: Revenue FY26
rows.delta: +5% cc; conservative vs Q1-26 +22% order print

rows.label: EBITDA margin FY26
rows.delta: +240bp YoY; operating leverage from 30-35% incremental margins

rows.label: FY27 modal EPS
rows.delta: if recovery sustains; multiple normalises to ~25x

blocks.text: _3Y TSR: bull CHF 305 → CHF 375-450 (+23-48%) on FY27 EPS 15-18 × 25x; base CHF 220-265 (-13 to -28%); bear CHF 96-128 (-58 to -68%) on cycle stall + AEIS confirmed share loss. R/R: 1:2 unfavourable at current price._

items.text: Bull TSR: FY27 EPS CHF 15-18 × 25x P/E = CHF 375-450 (+23-48% from CHF 305). Requires WFE sustains + AEIS not taking share + IXS stabilises.
Base TSR: FY27 EPS CHF 10-12 × 22x = CHF 220-265 (-13 to -28%). Recovery insufficient to justify current multiple.
Bear TSR: FY27 EPS CHF 6-8 × 16x = CHF 96-128 (-58 to -68%). WFE stalls + AEIS confirmed share loss + CHF appreciates.
**R/R asymmetric DOWNSIDE at CHF 305.** Bull case +23-48% requires multiple converging; bear case -58-68% requires only one of three triggers (cycle stall OR AEIS share loss OR CHF).

sub.text: Stock has rallied +82% from trough; margin of safety has narrowed.

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: PROGRESS to ESA — but with explicit acknowledgement that ESA work is GATED on RESEARCHER backfill (7 queries). Conviction C — forming. WFE recovery in orders confirmed; AEIS competitive question is the single highest-impact ESA priority. Sub-sweet-spot mkt cap structural sizing constraint.

blocks.text: _PROGRESS to ESA. WFE recovery confirmed in orders (Q1-26 +22.3% / B2B 1.4); margin recovery path arithmetically credible. But AEIS competitive divergence (+21% vs +7.3% cc FY25) raises structural-share-loss question that gates the case. Sub-sweet-spot mkt cap caps R/R._

items.text: **Why PROGRESS:** Q1-26 order inflection + AI/semi theme alignment + new CEO/CFO + technical setup B (11/11 Minervini) + sector cohort RS supportive.

sub.text: Material ESA-readiness gap (RESEARCHER queue not run) is operationally addressable; not analytically disqualifying.

items.text: **Why NOT progress directly to DD:** (1) AEIS competitive structural risk unresolved — ACH#8 WATCH; (2) Sub-sweet-spot mkt cap (~$1.3bn) caps position sizing per APM SKILL §6c (max 1.0%);

sub.text: (3) Late-stage technical entry (extended +28% from 200D, base-3) — wait for retest CHF 275-285.

items.text: **Three new ESA-priority findings driving the workplan:** (1) AEIS competitive dynamics — 14pp FY25 divergence is the single highest-impact unresolved question; (2) Synertia adoption volume —

sub.text: drives 100-200bp margin upside if works; (3) Sell-side coverage gap — could be inefficiency or disinterest, ESA via 06-SS deep query.

items.text: **What's at C-conviction:** Two-setup case (Demand-Driven EPSU + Corporate Change EPSU/EPT). Both setups present but contingent on H1-26 sustaining + AEIS resolution.

sub.text: R/R 1:2 unfavourable at CHF 305 (stock +82% from trough).

items.text: **ESA workplan:** RESEARCHER briefs filed for 7 queries; ESA APM A&J authoring deferred pending RESEARCHER content; estimated ESA-readiness in 2-3 weeks.

blocks.text: _Triaging-stage work surfaces the key Triaging-to-ESA gating question (AEIS competitive dynamics) clearly. ESA work, when ready, will resolve up or down decisively. Not a high-conviction PROGRESS but a defensible one — coverage gap at SS + structural sizing constraint mean ESA work needs to materially de-risk before DD-stage capital deployment makes sense._

C.title: REQUIRED ATTRIBUTES
C.inline_verdict: 6 pillars rated. P1 B (technical), P2 C (paradigm mixed), P3 C (case forming), P4 C (BB#5 D structural sizing constraint), P5 C (coverage gap), P6 C (R/R asymmetric down). Distribution: 0A / 1B (P1) / 4C / 0D / 0F at pillar level; 1B / 6C / 1D at family level (BB#5 D).

rows.summary: 11/11 Minervini + sector-supportive cohort + golden cross. Late-stage entry timing (extended +28% from 200D, base-3). Optimal retest CHF 275-285.

blocks.text: _Per RESEARCHER 03-TM/synthesis: Minervini MM99=11/11, Stage 2 advance, golden cross confirmed, RS 81st percentile universe / 90th sector. Stock +82% from 52W low — late-stage entry timing. Capital Probing Bet fails by 2.12pp (need breakout above CHF 317.88)._

items.text: **MM99 score 11/11.** All 8 Minervini criteria pass + 3 extension criteria. SP > 50D > 150D > 200D, all MAs rising, 200D up >1M, RS 81st percentile.

sub.text: Filter qualification: __Capital stage__. RS rank 81st universe / 90th sector — top-quartile leadership.
Stage 2 advance confirmed (Weinstein methodology). Golden cross 50D × 200D confirmed Q1-26.

items.text: **RS excess returns:** RS line breaking out above 52W high — top-quartile leadership signal. Sector cohort (semi-cap) RS rank ~70 — sector-supportive (not isolated like EKTA's med-tech).

sub.text: Sector strength HEALTHY — semi-cap equipment cohort (ASML, AMAT, LRCX, AEIS, COMET) trending up; cycle inflection visible across cohort. Different from EKTA's sectorally-isolated outperformance.

items.text: **Moving average structure:** 10/20/50/150/200 DMA aligned positive, all rising. Distance from 200D = +28% — getting __extended__ (>30% threshold for caution).

sub.text: Slope of 50D = +0.22%/day (steep rising); 200D = +0.08% (slow but rising). Late-stage acceleration pattern.
__Extended-from-200D warning__: Minervini doctrine — pullback to 50D likely before sustainable breakout above CHF 317.88 resistance.

items.text: **Base count:** Currently in __3rd-stage base__ since Mar-23 low. Each base higher and tighter. Bullish for early-cycle play; statistically late for continuation.

sub.text: Base 1 (Mar-23 → Jul-24): 16-month, depth -42%. Base 2 (Aug-24 → Dec-25): 16-month, depth -22%. Base 3 (Jan-26 → present): 4-month so far, depth -8%.

items.text: **Volume patterns:** OBV rising; 20D volume +25% vs 200D average — accumulation confirmed. No distribution days (3+ in 5W) since Jan-26 retest.
**Synthesis: Technical setup B — structurally sound but tactically late.** 11/11 Minervini + sector-supportive cohort + golden cross = institutional participation confirmed.

sub.text: But base-3 + extended-from-200D + Capital Probing Bet fails by 2.12pp = entry-timing-not-now.
Optimal entry retest: CHF 275-285 (50D pullback). Capital Probing Bet trigger: breakout above CHF 317.88 on volume.
__Late-stage entry implies smaller initial position size__ if/when fundamentals progress to entry — Trial slug only.

blocks.text: _P1 Technical = B. 11/11 Minervini, sector-supportive (semi-cap cohort), but extended +28% from 200D and base-3 = late-stage entry timing. Optimal retest CHF 275-285; Capital Probing Bet trigger CHF 317.88._

_The case is a DEMAND-DRIVEN EPSU on WFE cyclical recovery, with CORPORATE CHANGE EPSU/EPT secondary. P3 = C (outputs visible but contested by AEIS dynamics + CHF). P4 = C (foundation challenged by AEIS structural threat). Two material rating-revision candidates at ESA: (a) BB#2 Foundation Quality re-rate based on AEIS resolution; (b) IC#3 Setup re-rate based on H1-26 revenue tracking._

blocks.label: P3: Fundamental Investment Case (Outputs + Inputs + Setups)

blocks.text: _P3 = C. Three families (IC#1 OUTPUTS, IC#2 INPUTS, IC#3 SETUPS). All at C; IC#2.TC3 (competitive intensity) at D — the AEIS competitive intelligence question is the #1 ESA priority._

_Output bridge: PCT recovery (CHF 247m → 280-310m FY26) + IXS stabilisation (breakeven FY26) + margin expansion (10.1% → 12.5% modal FY26) = EPS recovery from -3.71 to +5. Mechanically credible BUT heavily contingent on H2-26 sustaining the H1-26 momentum._

items.text: **IC#1 family verdict: C.** Output bridge is mechanically real but H1-only inflection insufficient — case requires H2-26 sustaining for FY26 to track CHF 480m.

sub.text: Triple-recovery levers: (i) PCT volume from WFE recovery (+12-25% YoY feasible), (ii) IXS turnaround (CHF -12m → 0 EBITDA), (iii) Operating leverage from 30-35% incremental margins.

items.text: **TC1 EPS growth path credible? C.** FY26 consensus CHF 11 implies 3x recovery from FY25 trough — aggressive. Watson modal CHF 5 more conservative.

sub.text: Bull DNB-equivalent: FY27 EPS CHF 15-18 (mid-cycle peak). Bear: FY27 EPS CHF 6-8 (cycle stall + AEIS).

items.text: **TC2 Margin target 14-16% achievable? C.** Operating leverage path real but assumes 30-35% incremental margins consistently AND CHF stabilises AND AEIS doesn't take pricing.

sub.text: H2-25 +300bp HoH inflection is the single supportive data point; needs 2-3 more quarters to validate.

items.text: **TC3 FCF sustainable? B.** Penang capex peaks FY25 (CHF 42.5m); declines to ~CHF 20m FY26 → CHF 15m run-rate. FCF normalises CHF 20-50m FY26-27. Net debt manageable.

blocks.text: _Inputs: WFE recovery + AI-driven capex + new CEO/CFO + Penang capacity + IXS restructuring. Most are present and verifiable; AEIS competitive dynamics is the contested input._

items.text: **IC#2 family verdict: C.** Five of six required inputs present and verifiable; AEIS competitive intelligence remains contested.

sub.text: Inputs present: (1) Q1-26 WFE recovery ✓, (2) TSMC $52-56bn capex ✓, (3) New CEO/CFO ✓, (4) Penang capacity ramp ✓, (5) IXS restructuring ✓. Contested: (6) AEIS competitive dynamics ✗.

items.text: **TC1 Operator credible + decisive? B.** Haferl 3.5y in role; correct calls on Penang + Synertia + dividend cut. CFO Witt fresh from LPKF (cost-discipline reputation).

sub.text: Capital allocation: 'invest through trough' philosophy — capex when cheap, dividend cut when FCF compressed (correct), no M&A distractions (correct).

items.text: **TC2 Demand inputs intact? B.** WFE cycle stage = early-mid recovery; AI-driven logic/HBM capex pulling forward; TSMC + Samsung + Intel capex confirmed accelerating.

**TC3 Competitive intensity stable? D.** AEIS divergence FY25 (+21% vs COMET +7.3% cc) = 14pp gap. AEIS new platforms (eVoS, eVerest, NavX) competitive responses to Synertia. STRUCTURAL SHARE LOSS RISK.

sub.text: Bull explanation: 14pp gap is FX + customer mix timing. Bear: AEIS taking permanent share at leading nodes (ASML, Lam advanced).
__This is the #1 ESA-priority question__ — single most material gap in the case at Triaging stage.

blocks.text: _Two setups identified: Demand-Driven EPSU (WFE recovery, primary 70% weight) + Corporate Change EPSU/EPT (CEO/CFO refresh + Penang + IXS, secondary 30%). Both present at Triaging; both contingent on H1-26 evidence sustaining._

items.text: **IC#3 family verdict: C.** Two-setup case both present; bull thesis requires both setups to deliver simultaneously over 18-24M.

sub.text: Demand-Driven EPSU (primary): WFE +22% Q1-26 orders + 1.4 book-to-bill — present. Risk: H2-26 sustains.
Corporate Change EPSU/EPT (secondary): CEO refresh + Penang + IXS turnaround. Present and tracking; FY27+ benefit.

items.text: **TC1 Fulcrum driver identified + sized? C.** Fulcrum = WFE cycle sustaining into H2-26+ (not just H1 spring spike). Single binary variable.

sub.text: If WFE sustains: FY26 revenue CHF 480m+ achievable; bull case in play. If WFE stalls H2-26: revenue plateaus CHF 460m, margin recovery stalls.

items.text: **TC2 Order book quality validated? B.** Q1-26 +22.3% YoY at book-to-bill 1.4. Composition (PCT vs IXS) suggests recovery is broad-based not single-customer.
**TC3 Catalyst calendar visible? B.** Q1-26 print already (+22.3%); H1-26 results May-26; Q3-26 confirms H2 sustainability; CMD update Nov-26 (1y after credibility-cut CMD).

blocks.text: _P4 = C. 8 building blocks. Distribution: 1 B (BB#3 Mgmt); 6 C (BB#1, BB#2, BB#4, BB#6, BB#7, BB#8); 1 D (BB#5 Small Size). The BB#5 D is structural (sub-sweet-spot mkt cap)._
_Headline simplicity (WFE recovery + cost discipline) holds; ESA depth needed on AEIS competitive read + CHF FX permanence to confirm B vs C._

items.text: **BB#1 verdict: C.** Headline case comprehensible; sub-surface complexity around AEIS dynamics + CHF FX stress-testing + Synertia adoption volume.

sub.text: Hidden complexity: AEIS share question + Synertia unproven volume + CHF permanent drag + IXS recovery path uncertainty.

items.text: **RA1 (DOUBLE per v3.5/3.8) Required simplicity guardrails.** Three guardrails to hold: (i) WFE sustains, (ii) AEIS doesn't take share, (iii) CHF stable. Conjunction probability ~30%.

blocks.text: _BB#2 is QUADRUPLED-floor at Triaging (per memo doctrine §IV.F). Three Foundation issues warrant ESA depth: (i) AEIS competitive structure, (ii) duopoly→3-player erosion (NAURA, AMEC Chinese entrants), (iii) Synertia moat durability._

items.text: **BB#2 family verdict: C.** Foundation has durable elements (75y vacuum capacitor tribal knowledge, 6-18M qualification barriers) AND eroding elements (AEIS bundle/scale, Chinese entrants).

sub.text: Durable: 75y vacuum capacitor design, irreplaceable in high-precision RF; 6-18M re-qualification cycles.
Eroding: AEIS scale + diversification (data centre); Chinese NAURA/AMEC at lower-spec; Synertia adoption unproven.

items.text: **TC1 Industry structure stable? C.** WFE component supply 3-player duopoly+ (AEIS + COMET + MKSI). Stable at duopoly level; share __within__ duopoly slipping toward AEIS.

sub.text: AEIS ~45% RF power share, COMET ~30%, MKSI ~25% (illustrative; ESA needs precise number).

items.text: **TC2 Switching costs / installed base sticky? B.** Qualification barriers high; OEM re-qualification 6-18M; tribal knowledge moat real.
**TC3 ROIC / capital efficiency? C.** ROIC depressed at trough (~2-3%); recovers to 8-12% mid-cycle. Penang capex ROI to be validated FY27+.
**TC4 Competitive moat trajectory? D — DETERIORATING.** AEIS gaining; Chinese entrants emerging; Synertia adoption critical to maintain position.

sub.text: Worst-case scenario: AEIS takes 5pp share + Chinese entrants take 3pp at lower-spec = COMET PCT structurally peaks at CHF 280m, not CHF 380m historic peak.
__Per APM SKILL §Mediocre Quality + Weak Inflection = SELL__: COMET is on the edge of this rule. Foundation Quality C/D with H1-only inflection is the textbook pattern.

blocks.text: _CEO Haferl + CFO Witt + refreshed board (Loh chair, ASMI ex-CEO; Boudre industrial tech) = governance positive. CFO transparency in CMD scenario analysis = improving discipline._

items.text: **BB#3 family verdict: B.** Operator quality credible (semiconductor-experienced); CFO refresh adds discipline; board independent and refreshed Apr-26.

sub.text: Haferl: 3.5y CEO, ex-X-ray business head COMET, semiconductor-literate; correct strategic calls on Penang + Synertia + IXS + dividend cut.
Witt: Jan-25 CFO, ex-LPKF Laser 28y, cost-discipline reputation; CMD scenario analysis = positive transparency signal.

items.text: **RA1 Forecasting transparency? C.** FY24 ✓ FY25 ✗ CMD-cut. Mixed track record; FY26 vague guidance is defensive.

blocks.text: _Strenuously seek-to-avoid: cyclical exposure (WFE 1.5-2.0x beta) + structural share loss risk (AEIS) + permanent FX drag (CHF). Two of three avoid-patterns elevated; case is on the edge._

items.text: **BB#4 family verdict: C.** Two avoid-patterns present (high cyclicality + AEIS structural share threat); third (FX drag) is permanent constraint, not invalidating.

sub.text: Cyclical: WFE 1.5-2.0x beta in downturns; bear-market drawdown -25-35% before fundamentals deteriorate.
Structural: AEIS share threat + Chinese entrant emergence.
FX: CHF permanent drag, unhedgeable, -CHF 2.5-3.0m revenue / 1% appreciation.

blocks.text: _COTN-CH mkt cap ~CHF 1.2bn ≈ USD 1.3bn — BELOW Richard's $5-50bn sweet spot floor. Liquidity adequate (Swiss listed, ADV decent) but size constraint is real._

items.text: **BB#5 verdict: D.** Mkt cap ~$1.3bn is materially below sweet-spot floor of $5bn. Sub-sweet-spot stocks carry higher single-stock risk for the same conviction.

sub.text: Implication for sizing: max 1.0% per APM SKILL §6c (vs 2.5% for in-sweet-spot names). This is a meaningful position-sizing constraint.
__This is the strongest single negative finding at Triaging__ — even with B-conviction, the size constraint caps R/R contribution to portfolio.

blocks.text: _BB#6 examines 10 INVALIDATION ACHs. 9 of 10 PASS; ACH#8 (AEIS structural share loss / narrow frame) is the ONE that is WATCH/UNRESOLVED. Plain sight risks (AEIS + CHF) flagged DOUBLE per v3.8 doctrine._

items.text: **BB#6 verdict: C.** 9 of 10 ACHs PASS at Triaging; ACH#8 (Narrow frame / AEIS share) is the single contested ACH. Plain sight risks RA elevated to DOUBLE-weight per v3.8.
**RA1 (DOUBLE per v3.8) Plain sight risks?** AEIS share loss + CHF appreciation = two plain-sight risks. Both visible; both contested between bull/bear narratives.

blocks.text: _3y operating trend: revenue trough FY23 (CHF 350m) → recovery FY25 (CHF 457m) → FY26E ~CHF 480m. EBITDA margin trough 6-8% → 10.1% FY25 → 12-13% modal FY26. Inflection real but recent (1Y) — not yet a trend._

items.text: **BB#7 verdict: C.** Operating-trend inflection real but short (1Y); needs 3-4 more clean prints to confirm structural inflection vs cycle bounce.

sub.text: Revenue cc CAGR FY23-25: +14%/yr (trough recovery). EBITDA margin: FY23 ~7% → FY25 10.1% (+310bp). Two-year trend positive.
__Two prints (FY24 + FY25) with H2-25 inflection make a thesis, not a trend__. Need H1-26 + Q3-26 to validate structural shift.

blocks.text: _Optionality stack: Synertia platform adoption (high upside if works), Penang efficiency (FY27+), AI-data-centre adjacency via PCT, IXS turnaround as free option. Each individually low-probability or long-dated._

items.text: **BB#8 verdict: C.** Optionality stack real but each individually contingent or long-dated. Not pay-down on its own.

sub.text: Synertia volume adoption: high upside (deeper customer integration, higher ASP) but unproven; FY27+ event.
Penang efficiency: lower CHF cost exposure + Asian customer proximity. FY27+ benefit.
IXS breakeven FY26: free option (already in modal); upside if European industrial recovers faster.

blocks.text: _P2 = C. Sub-sweet-spot mkt cap (-) + AI/semi theme alignment (+) + sector cohort supportive (+) + Iran/risk-off non-aligned (-) + CHF FX hedge for EUR/GBP investors (+/-) = mixed paradigm fit._

items.text: **Me-state (a) — C.** Richard's UK/Europe coverage mandate fits Swiss-listed semi-cap. Adjacent sector experience (FEVR food, MTU industrial) — semi-cap is genuinely new territory.
**Sector / theme alignment (b) — B.** AI infrastructure (T2=B) — second-order beneficiary via WFE → semi → AI logic/HBM. Semi-cap cohort RS supportive (~70).
**Macro exposure (c) — C.** WFE cyclical 1.5-2.0x beta. Iran/risk-off non-aligned (T3=D). Bear market resilience low (T1=D).
**Portfolio overlap (d) — B.** No direct overlap with current squad (industrials-heavy). Semi-cap adds diversification.
**Sizing/liquidity (e) — D.** Mkt cap CHF 1.2bn ≈ USD 1.3bn — BELOW $5bn sweet-spot floor. ADV adequate but size constraint binding.
**Synthesis (f) — C.** Mixed paradigm fit: thematic + portfolio additive (positive); sizing + macro (negative). Net: paradigm-fit-OK-with-frictions.

blocks.text: _P2 = C. AI semi theme alignment + portfolio diversification positive; sub-sweet-spot mkt cap + Iran-non-aligned + bear-market-vulnerable negative._

_P5 = C. SS coverage thin (4-6 active analysts; AS query returned no broker data — coverage gap). Implicit consensus FY26 EPS CHF 11; revisions trajectory unknown from RESEARCHER. Coverage gap = potential inefficiency or disinterest._

items.text: **Coverage breadth:** 4-6 active analysts (Vontobel, ZKB, BoA, Kepler — partially confirmed). Thin for a $1.3bn Swiss mid-cap.
**Implicit consensus FY26:** EPS CHF 11 (post-CMD reset); FY27 EPS CHF 10-12. Wide implicit dispersion not visible due to coverage gap.
**Revisions trajectory unknown** — needs ESA-stage 06-SS deep query. RESEARCHER 06-SS Triaging file is thin; coverage gap is the gate.
**Synthesis: C.** Coverage gap is double-edged — could signal inefficiency (research arbitrage) or disinterest (illiquid mid-cap). Triaging cannot resolve.

blocks.text: _P5 = C. SS coverage thin; ESA-stage 06-SS deep query is the gate to resolving consensus + dispersion + congruence questions._

_P6 = C. NTM P/E 28x (mid-cycle range; vs trough 12-18x); EV/EBITDA 20x peak; vs peer AEIS 25-30x, VAT 56x, MKSI 15-20x. Stock +82% from trough — re-rating already occurred. R/R 1:2 unfavourable at CHF 305._

items.text: **Primary metric:** NTM P/E 28x (on consensus FY26 EPS CHF 11). Direction: re-rated from 12-18x trough → 28x mid-cycle range. EV/EBITDA 20x peak.
**Percentile vs own history:** P/E 28x = 50th percentile of recovery range (12-45x range). Vs peer group: 40-45th percentile.
**Qualitative R/R:** Discount/premium contested. AEIS 25-30x = peer benchmark. COMET premium would require AEIS-resolution-favourable.
**Synthesis: C.** Stock has rallied +82% from trough; margin of safety has narrowed. R/R 1:2 unfavourable at current price.

blocks.text: _P6 = C. Mid-cycle valuation; R/R asymmetric DOWNSIDE at CHF 305; entry would require pullback to CHF 275-285 (P1 retest target)._

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: 5 KDIC + 3 KHF + 4 KCs + 6 KRs + 3 KPOs. ACHs: 9 of 10 PASS; ACH#8 (Narrow frame / AEIS) WATCH. Confidence MEDIUM; AEIS competitive intelligence is the single highest-confidence-impact gap.

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: **KDIC#1 (PRIMARY) WFE cycle sustaining into H2-26+.** Q1-26 +22.3% / B2B 1.4 confirmed; gate is H2-26 not just spring spike.
**KDIC#2 (PRIMARY) Margin expansion via operating leverage.** 30-35% incremental margins; H2-25 +300bp HoH supportive print.
**KDIC#3 (SECONDARY) IXS breakeven FY26.** Restructuring done CHF 15-20m; dependent on European industrial stabilisation.
**KDIC#4 (SECONDARY) Synertia adoption volume.** Higher ASP + customer integration; unproven at volume; FY27+ event.
**KDIC#5 (TERTIARY) CHF/USD stabilises < 0.95.** External, not in mgmt control; -CHF 2.5-3m revenue / 1% appreciation.
**KHF#1:** No further material guidance miss in FY26 (third miss = credibility broken). Probability ~70%.
**KHF#2:** Haferl + Witt remain in role; no surprise leadership transition. Probability ~95%.
**KHF#3:** Net debt remains <2.5x EBITDA; no equity raise. Probability ~90%.
**KC#1 (HIGHEST IMPACT):** AEIS structural share loss vs cyclical timing? — resolves at ESA via 12-VCAnalysis + primary research.
**KC#2:** Synertia volume adoption trajectory? — resolves at ESA via 14-KD deep + customer reference channels.
**KC#3:** Sell-side coverage gap = inefficiency or disinterest? — resolves at ESA via 06-SS deep query + new initiations tracker.
**KC#4:** IXS European recovery pace? — resolves H2-26 print + FY27 industrial cycle.
**KR#1 (HIGH IMPACT × MED PROB):** AEIS confirmed structural share loss at leading nodes → COMET PCT peak structurally lower.
**KR#2 (HIGH IMPACT × MED PROB):** WFE cycle stalls H2-26 (AEIS already cautious on H2 FY26).
**KR#3 (MED IMPACT × HIGH PROB):** CHF appreciates to 0.95-1.00 — additional CHF 2.5-3m revenue headwind / 1% move; persistent.
**KR#4 (MED IMPACT × MED PROB):** IXS stays loss-making FY26 (European industrial recovery delayed).
**KR#5 (MED IMPACT × LOW PROB):** Synertia adoption stalls below volume threshold for unit-economics breakeven.
**KR#6 (LOW IMPACT × HIGH PROB):** Continued sub-sweet-spot mkt cap caps R/R contribution to portfolio.
**KPO#1:** AEIS resolution favourable (COMET holding share via Synertia adoption) → conviction up B/Green; multiple expansion.
**KPO#2:** WFE super-cycle 2027-28 (AI capex compounds) → COMET PCT peak revenue CHF 380-400m vs CMD 670-770m group target.
**KPO#3:** Penang efficiency + Asian customer proximity FY27+ → margin uplift +100-150bp + customer wins.

blocks.text: Per APM SKILL §10 INVALIDATION ACHs (Locked 21-Apr-26): live-case invalidation thresholds. Mandatory at every stage. COTN-CH-specific thresholds below.

items.text: **ACH#1 Top-line invalidation:** PASS. Q1-26 orders +22.3% YoY; book-to-bill 1.4. Trigger: orders <0% YoY for 2 consecutive quarters.
**ACH#2 Cockroach invalidation:** PASS. PCT GM ~38%; no margin collapse signal. Trigger: PCT GM <30% AND IXS losses widen.
**ACH#3 Ditherer invalidation:** WATCH. Operator decisive (CEO Haferl + CFO Witt) but FY25 miss + CMD downgrade = mixed track record. Trigger: FY26 miss >5% (third strike).
**ACH#4 Cyclical invalidation:** PASS. SP +82% from trough (no underperformance). Trigger: SP -15% in 3M + WFE cycle reversal evidence.
**ACH#5 NT/MT one-two:** PASS. SP outperforming; FY26 trajectory tracking. Trigger: SP -15% + FY26 revenue growth <5%.
**ACH#6 Wisdom of crowd:** PASS. Trigger: SP -15% in 3M + plausible mid-term concerns on AEIS share or margin.
**ACH#7 Existential:** PASS. Net debt 2.1x EBITDA; manageable. Trigger: net debt >3.0x OR FX loss >CHF 10m.

**ACH#8 Narrow frame invalidation: WATCH — UNRESOLVED.** AEIS competitive divergence (FY25: AEIS +21% vs COMET +7.3% cc = 14pp gap). Trigger: AEIS continues outpacing COMET 10pp+ AND COMET PCT revenue

sub.text: plateaus <CHF 280m.

items.text: **ACH#9 SS EEG invalidation:** PASS. Coverage gap; no widespread SS downgrades. Trigger: ≥2% SS EPS cuts AND SP -15%.
**ACH#10 Case outputs/attributes:** PASS. Current rating distribution: 1 D (BB#5 structural sizing); 6 C; 1 B. Trigger: 8+ at D or F.
**ACH#11 (NEW for COTN-CH) CHF appreciation invalidation:** WATCH. CHF/USD currently ~0.85; appreciation to 0.95-1.00 = -CHF 8-12m EBITDA / year (unhedgeable). Trigger: CHF/USD <0.90.
**Stock-specific thresholds (if position were held):** (1) PCT revenue <CHF 250m FY26, (2) Q3-26 orders YoY negative, (3) FY26 EBITDA margin <11%, (4) CHF/USD <0.90.
**Archetype-specific sell rule:** Per Wisdom Library 'Cyclical Recovery Trades' — set 6M milestone review. If H1-26 revenue tracking below CHF 240m, downgrade conviction C → D.
**Confidence: MEDIUM.** WFE recovery in orders high-confidence (>85%); margin path medium (~60% — assumes AEIS doesn't take pricing); operator-quality medium-high (~70% —

sub.text: Haferl track record solid but FY25 miss is real).

items.text: **Gap #1 — Notion/RNTS not consulted.** No Notion connector this session; Richard's-own-voice notes not integrated. Per AJ SOP §Phase 1 input #3 doctrinally-required gap flag.
**Gap #2 — AEIS competitive intelligence MISSING.** Public data insufficient to resolve structural-vs-cyclical share loss question. ESA-stage gap; needs primary research at DD.
**Gap #3 — Synertia volume adoption MISSING.** RESEARCHER coverage doesn't include customer reference checks. ESA-stage gap; needs primary research.
**Gap #4 — Sell-side coverage thin.** AS query returned no broker data; ESA-stage 06-SS deep query is the gate.

**Gap #5 — ESA RESEARCHER queue NOT YET RUN.** All 7 ESA queries (08-BM, 09-Earnings, 10-Short, 11-VCMap, 12-VCAnalysis, 13-Guidance, 14-KD) missing; brief filed to memory/staging/researcher-queue.md.

blocks.text: _Negatives expected between now and FY26 H1 print May-26:_

items.text: **FY26 guidance update H1 print:** Some quarter-on-quarter volatility; H2-25 +300bp HoH inflection may not extend uniformly to H1-26.
**SS coverage gap may persist:** Limited research could continue if mid-cap interest stays low; potential for upside surprise on positive print.
**CHF/USD FX:** Continued 100-200bp drag through FY26 unless USD strengthens or interest-rate differential narrows.
**IXS:** Continued losses likely H1-26; breakeven targeted H2-26.
**KOQ#1:** Is AEIS taking COMET share structurally or just cyclically? Resolves at ESA (12-VCAnalysis) + DD (primary).
**KOQ#2:** Is the WFE cycle inflection sustaining into H2-26+? Resolves H1-26 print + Q3-26 orders.
**KOQ#3:** Can Synertia achieve volume adoption to lift PCT margin? Resolves over 12-18M of design-win + volume-ramp data.
**KOQ#4:** Will sell-side coverage expand if FY26 delivers? Resolves H1-26 print + initiation tracking.
**KOQ#5:** What's the IXS terminal value scenario? Sale, fix, or stay? Resolves at June-26 strategic update.
**KA#1:** PROGRESS to ESA. Brief RESEARCHER on full ESA queue (7 queries: 08-BM, 09-Earnings, 10-Short, 11-VCMap, 12-VCAnalysis, 13-Guidance, 14-KD).
**KA#2:** Highest priority ESA query: 12-VCAnalysis with explicit AEIS competitive dynamics deep-dive.
**KA#3:** Add COTN-CH to fortnightly monitoring with H1-26 revenue tracking (target >CHF 240m), CHF/USD FX monitor, AEIS quarterly results.
**KA#4:** Schedule operator-quality calibration via track-record cross-check: Haferl's prior X-ray business head tenure + decisions made.

E.title: ACTIONS
E.inline_verdict: PROGRESS to ESA. ESA RESEARCHER queue NOT YET RUN — brief filed. Highest-priority ESA work: AEIS competitive intelligence (12-VCAnalysis deep) — single most material question for the case.

subsections.title: Pros/cons of prioritising vs other opportunities

items.text: **Pros (vs current pipeline):** (1) AI/semi theme alignment via WFE recovery is rare in European mid-cap. (2) Q1-26 +22.3% order print = strongest single-quarter signal in cohort.

sub.text: (3) Technical setup B (11/11 Minervini); entry timing window opens if pullback retest. (4) CFO refresh + board independence = governance upgrade visible.

items.text: **Cons (vs current pipeline):** (1) Sub-sweet-spot mkt cap (~$1.3bn) caps R/R contribution. (2) AEIS competitive structural risk unresolved — ACH#8 WATCH. (3) CHF FX permanent drag.

sub.text: (4) ESA RESEARCHER queue NOT YET RUN — no analytical readiness vs PRY (full ESA done) or HTRO (full ESA done).

items.text: **APM RECOMMENDATION:** PROGRESS to ESA — but with explicit acknowledgement that ESA-stage work is GATED on RESEARCHER backfill (7 queries) before APM A&J can proceed at ESA depth.
**vs alternatives:** PRY ESA already done (PROGRESS to DD recommended); HTRO ESA already done (PROGRESS to DD with cockroach flag); EKTA ESA already done (PARK).

sub.text: COTN-CH ESA work is materially behind — 7 RESEARCHER queries to run before APM A&J ESA can be authored.
